channel.title: archive on New Wayland
channel.description: Recent content in archive on New Wayland

item.title: It's the Exporters Stupid
item.description: A very good article here that comes closer than most to what I consider the crunch issue. The key point is that if a currency moves down so that imports become ‘more expensive’, then the ‘inflation’ that goes off is a distributional response that tries to eliminate some of those imports so that the exchange demands equalise. That also eliminates somebody else’s exports. The important thing to remember is that when a currency goes down, all the others in the world go up in relation to it and nations that rely upon exports (export led nations) start to lose trade - which depresses their own economy.

item.title: Post War Banking Policy
item.description: From the archives: Post War Banking Policy - Reginald McKenna

item.title: Savings Are an Export Product
item.description: Things make a lot more sense when you realise other currency areas love your savings products — a lot

item.title: Labour Hours and Labour Services
item.description: Employment is about buying the former and generating the latter. How does that relate to the Job Guarantee?

item.title: Basic Income: Thief of Time
item.description: Basic income is a desire to be paid twice - once self consuming your own hours and once consuming the output of somebody else’s hours

item.title: Job Guarantee: Jobs for the People
item.description: How the Job Guarantee solves the matching problem

item.title: This is how You Abolish Tuition Fees
item.description: Student debt is another set of debt that isn’t really a debt. So just cancel it all
